title: pimelea leucantha
intro: Pimelea leucantha, also known as the white rice flower, is a shrub native to Australia that belongs to the Thymelaeaceae family. The leaves and flowers of this plant have been used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Pimelea leucantha include the potential to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its use as an antiseptic to prevent wound infections. In herbal preparations, the leaves and flowers can be made into teas, tinctures, or infusions, and can also be used topically as a poultice or salve to aid in wound healing.
